title: Datadog Cloud-SIEM signal-history triage outcome
description: One record per Datadog Cloud-SIEM / Security-Monitoring SIGNAL that fired and its triage outcome: the signal's id, the firing rule's id + name, severity, the triage status (open / under_review / triaged / archived), the timeline timestamps (first-seen / triaged / last-updated), and the OPAQUE handle of who triaged. Pulled read-only over a bounded look-back window from the Datadog security-signals search API (GET /api/v2/security_monitoring/signals, security_monitoring_signals_read scope). This is the slice-636 sibling of datadog.siem_rule.v1 (slice 533): slice 533 reads detection-rule CONFIGURATION (SOC 2 CC7.2 — which rules exist); this surface reads what FIRED and how it was TRIAGED (SOC 2 CC7.3 — incident response: rules actually fired and were triaged over the audit period, when, and by whom). Triage METADATA only — NEVER the signal MESSAGE body, the matched log/event SAMPLES, the matched-event payload, the raw detection query, signal-body tags/facets, or a recipient/triager email address (PII); the connector's record struct has no field that can hold them and a reflection guard fails the build if one is added. The connector reports a descriptive triage posture; the platform evaluator owns the final pass/fail per (control, scope). Profile: bounded PULL on the operator's schedule — NOT continuous monitoring and NOT event-driven; the platform-side wire is always push.

signal_id.description: Datadog-native stable identifier of the security signal (non-secret; an opaque ID, not a credential).

rule_id.description: Datadog-native identifier of the detection rule that fired this signal — ties the signal back to its datadog.siem_rule.v1 configuration record (slice 533).

rule_name.description: Human-readable name of the firing detection rule (descriptive label; never the signal message body).

severity.description: The signal's severity label (info / low / medium / high / critical). A descriptive label of the rule's configured severity reflected on the signal, NEVER the matched content.

status.description: The signal's triage status, normalized to the audit vocabulary: 'open' (un-triaged), 'under_review' (assigned/being reviewed), 'triaged' (an explicit triage disposition), or 'archived' (Datadog's native closed state). Derived from Datadog's workflow triage_state; an unknown state normalizes to 'open' (the conservative audit-relevant case).

first_seen_at.description: RFC3339 timestamp of when the signal was first observed (the firing instant). Proves the detection happened within the audit period.

triaged_at.description: RFC3339 timestamp of when the signal's triage state was last updated. Proves a triage action happened within the audit period.

last_updated_at.description: RFC3339 timestamp of the signal's most recent workflow update.

triager_handle.description: The OPAQUE handle / id of who triaged the signal (the archiver, falling back to the assignee). A handle or stable id only — NEVER a raw email address or display name (PII); an email-shaped value is dropped before this field is populated.
